cuisine: Clinical Medical Nutrition — Food for Special Medical Purposes (FSMP)
region_of_origin: Netherlands (Nutricia/Danone Medical Nutrition); distributed in Singapore through hospital oncology dietitians
food_subgroup: High-EPA oncology oral nutrition supplement (FSMP); EPA 1.44g per 100ml (1.8g per 125ml bottle); indicated for cancer cachexia attenuation and pre-surgical immunonutrition

ontology.foodon_gap_note: No FoodOn URI exists for a specific oncology FSMP with high-EPA enrichment. Closest parent concept: oral nutritional supplement, EPA-enriched, oncology indication (FSMP). FoodOn does not currently enumerate disease-specific medical nutrition products at this level of specificity.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 does not enumerate individual branded FSMP products or specific disease-state formulations. Closest parent 'Oral nutritional supplement' (A0002) used.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT code for this specific branded FSMP. Closest parent concept 'Oral nutritional supplement product' (226010008) noted.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard serving is one 125ml compact bottle of Forticare, providing approximately 250 kcal, 10g protein, 14.6g fat, 20g carbohydrate, and 1.8g EPA omega-3 fatty acid; ESPEN 2021 recommends 2–3 bottles per day for 5–7 days pre-surgery as immunonutrition.

nutrient_source.title: Forticare — Nutricia Singapore Product Specification 2024 and ESPEN Clinical Nutrition Guideline for Oncology 2021
nutrient_source.organization: Nutricia (Danone Medical Nutrition), Singapore; European Society for Clinical Nutrition and Metabolism (ESPEN)
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values sourced directly from Nutricia Singapore product specification for Forticare (2024). All per-100ml values are manufacturer-verified. ESPEN 2021 oncology guideline cited for clinical indication and dosing recommendations. This is a branded FSMP product; manufacturer specification is the authoritative source per Project Veritas approved-source policy for packaged/commercial medical nutrition products (equivalent to USDA Branded Food tier for commercial products).

gi_source.title: Glycemic index estimated for Forticare oncology FSMP based on analogous maltodextrin-based oral nutritional supplement with protein and fat moderating effect; reference: University of Sydney GI Database — oral nutritional supplements and mixed macronutrient formulas
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement for Forticare exists in the University of Sydney GI Database. GI ~52 is an analogous estimate based on: (1) maltodextrin-based carbohydrate source (moderate GI ~60–65 in isolation); (2) significant protein content (8g/100ml) and fat content (11.7g/100ml) which substantially reduce postprandial glycemic response; (3) comparison with similar mixed-macronutrient oral nutritional supplements in the University of Sydney GI Database (GI range 33–55 for protein/fat-moderated ONS). GL per 125ml bottle = GI × (carbohydrate per serving / 100) = 52 × (20/100) = 10.4. Confidence: 0.9.

hypertension.key_factor: Low sodium content (80mg/100ml; ~100mg per 125ml bottle) combined with EPA omega-3 antihypertensive effect
hypertension.guidance: Forticare is suitable for oncology patients with comorbid hypertension; sodium per bottle (~100mg) is well within low-sodium dietary targets, and the EPA omega-3 content (1.8g per bottle) provides a secondary antihypertensive benefit by reducing arterial stiffness and improving endothelial function — monitor blood pressure as part of routine oncology care.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Moderate glycemic load (~10.4 per 125ml bottle) with EPA insulin-sensitising effect; T2DM-oncology comorbidity is clinically common
type2_diabetes.guidance: Forticare may be used in oncology patients with Type 2 Diabetes under dietitian supervision; the glycemic load per bottle is low-to-moderate (GL ~10.4), and EPA omega-3 has independent insulin-sensitising effects (reduces triglycerides, improves insulin receptor sensitivity), but blood glucose monitoring is recommended when using 2–3 bottles per day as per ESPEN immunonutrition protocol.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: High EPA omega-3 content (1.8g per bottle) with low saturated fat (2g/100ml) and low sodium; EPA has established cardioprotective effects
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Forticare is cardioprotective in the context of oncology nutrition support; the EPA omega-3 content (1.8g per 125ml bottle) reduces cardiovascular risk by lowering triglycerides, reducing platelet aggregation, and improving endothelial function, while saturated fat is low (2g/100ml) and sodium is low (80mg/100ml) — suitable for oncology patients with concurrent cardiovascular disease.

cancer_cachexia.key_factor: Primary indication: EPA 1.44g/100ml (1.8g per bottle) attenuates cancer-related muscle wasting and systemic inflammation
cancer_cachexia.guidance: Forticare is the primary FSMP indicated for cancer cachexia attenuation; ESPEN 2021 Grade A recommendation supports 2–3 bottles per day for patients with cancer cachexia, with EPA directly inhibiting pro-inflammatory cytokines (IL-6, TNF-alpha) that drive muscle protein catabolism.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: EPA content (1.8g/bottle) directly reduces arachidonic acid pathway hyperactivation in RA synovitis
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: For RA patients with concurrent cancer requiring Forticare, the high EPA content (1.8g per bottle) provides dual benefit — attenuating cancer cachexia and reducing RA synovial inflammation via competitive inhibition of the arachidonic acid pathway.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Forticare is a ready-to-drink oral nutritional supplement (ONS) classified as a Food for Special Medical Purposes (FSMP), manufactured by Nutricia (Danone Medical Nutrition) and distributed in Singapore through hospital oncology dietitians at institutions including NCC and SGH. It is available in a 125ml compact bottle and is specifically formulated for oncology patients. Per 100ml, Forticare provides approximately 200 kcal, 8g protein, 11.7g fat (of which 2g saturated fat), and 16g carbohydrate (10g sugar), with a sodium content of 80mg. Its defining clinical feature is a high EPA (eicosapentaenoic acid) omega-3 content of 1.44g per 100ml — equivalent to 1.8g EPA per 125ml bottle — which is approximately 3 times higher than standard oral nutritional supplements. This EPA concentration is clinically significant for two primary indications: (1) cancer cachexia attenuation, where EPA inhibits pro-inflammatory cytokines (IL-6, TNF-alpha) that drive muscle protein catabolism and weight loss in cancer patients; and (2) pre-surgical immunonutrition, for which ESPEN 2021 provides a Grade A recommendation of 2–3 bottles per day for 5–7 days before major oncological surgery to reduce post-operative complications and improve immune function. Forticare is gluten-free but contains fish oil (CRITICAL allergen for fish-allergic patients) and milk proteins (casein and whey). It is distinct from Supportan, another oncology ONS, which has lower EPA content and is used for general oncology nutritional support rather than high-dose EPA immunonutrition. The glycemic load per bottle is approximately 10.4 (low-to-moderate), making it manageable for oncology patients with comorbid Type 2 Diabetes under dietitian supervision. Forticare is the only widely-prescribed high-EPA oral nutritional supplement specifically designed for cancer cachexia and immunonutrition in Singapore. It has a neutral, slightly sweet taste, designed for palatability in patients with altered taste perception due to cancer or treatment.

confidence_reason: Nutrient values sourced directly from Nutricia Singapore manufacturer product specification (2024), which is the authoritative primary source for a branded FSMP product. Clinical indications and dosing supported by ESPEN 2021 oncology guideline (Grade A evidence). All core macros and micronutrients are manufacturer-verified with confidence 1.0. GI is an analogous estimate (confidence 0.9) as no direct GI measurement exists for this specific product.
notes: 1. NEGATIVE SENTINEL VALUES: monounsaturated_fat_g and polyunsaturated_fat_g are set to -1.0 as detailed fatty acid fractionation beyond total fat, saturated fat, and EPA is not reported in the Nutricia Singapore product specification for Forticare (2024). water_g is set to -1.0 as moisture content is not reported in the manufacturer specification. These values are not available in USDA FoodData Central or Singapore HPB database for this specific branded FSMP product. 2. DIETARY FIBRE: dietary_fibre_g is 0.0 — confirmed zero per Nutricia Singapore product specification; this is a liquid medical formula with no added dietary fibre. 3. TRANS FAT: trans_fat_g is 0.0 — confirmed zero per manufacturer specification. 4. GI ESTIMATION: GI of 52 is an analogous estimate based on mixed-macronutrient ONS comparators in the University of Sydney GI Database; no direct GI measurement for Forticare exists. 5. FISH ALLERGY: Forticare contains fish oil and is CONTRAINDICATED in patients with fish allergy — this is a critical clinical safety note. 6. HALAL STATUS: Halal certification status for Forticare in Singapore is not confirmed in the manufacturer specification reviewed; is_halal set to false pending verification. 7. MULTILINGUAL NAMES: Forticare is a branded clinical FSMP product, not a traditional food dish. While the product itself is not a 'Singaporean dish', for comprehensive multilingual coverage in a Singaporean context, common descriptive names in Chinese, Tamil, and Hindi have been added to local_names and aliases, reflecting how such a product might be referred to or searched for by different language groups in Singapore. Malay alias is from the input.
